The summary highlights that while direct data from the 2011 U.S. Census isn't present, existing reliable sources confirm long-standing gender disparities in lifespan—women living longer than men—which makes option A more plausible given its alignment with observed trends even if exact year-specific figures are missing.\boxed{\text{A}} Agent2’s reasoning focuses on identifying a lack of precise 2011 U.S. life expectancy data but relies instead on well-established demographic trends showing that women typically live longer than men.
